zotero.abstractNote: Pruning deep neural networks is a standard technique to reduce their computational footprint. While many pruning methods exist, they often rely on heuristics or require expensive retraining. In this work, we propose a novel meta-pruning approach that leverages optimal transport to learn a pruning policy. Our method, which we call Meta-Pruning via Optimal Transport (MPOT), learns to map the weights of a pre-trained network to a pruned version by minimizing the Wasserstein distance between their activation distributions. We demonstrate that MPOT achieves competitive performance on various benchmarks while being significantly more efficient than existing meta-pruning techniques.
zotero.conferenceName: The Twelfth International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: The Twelfth International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR)
